title: Using variables for sequence and object names
description:
Using variables for sequence and object names

Using Variables for Sequence and Object Names

String variables can be used for the calibration, sequence, and object name arguments in the VRun, VGet and VSet commands.
  The example below uses the string variables seq$ and obj$ to specify which vision sequence and which vision object to work with.

Function visTest

#define Z_PICK -100

		  String   seq$, obj$

		  Integer  found

		  Real  x, y, u

seq$ = "test"

		  obj$ = "blob01"

		  VSet seq$.Camera, 1

		  VSet seq$.Calibration, "CAMCAL1"

		  VRun seq$

		  VGet seq$.obj$.RobotXYU, found, x, y, u

		  If found Then

		    pick = XY(x, y, Z_PICK, u)

		    Jump pick

		    On vacuum

		    Wait .1

		    Jump place

		    Off vacuum

		    Wait .1

		  EndIf

		  Jump park

		Fend

Arrays can also be used for sequence and object name arguments in the VRun, VGet, and VSet commands.  See the example below.

Function test

		  String obj$(10)

		  obj$(0) = "corr01"

		  VRun seqname

		  VGet seqname.obj$(0).found, found

		Fend
examples:
Function visTest

#define Z_PICK -100
		  String   seq$, obj$
		  Integer  found
		  Real  x, y, u

seq$ = "test"
		  obj$ = "blob01"
		  VSet seq$.Camera, 1
		  VSet seq$.Calibration, "CAMCAL1"
		  VRun seq$
		  VGet seq$.obj$.RobotXYU, found, x, y, u
		  If found Then
		    pick = XY(x, y, Z_PICK, u)
		    Jump pick
		    On vacuum
		    Wait .1
		    Jump place
		    Off vacuum
		    Wait .1
		  EndIf
		  Jump park
		Fend

Function test
		  String obj$(10)
		  obj$(0) = "corr01"
		  VRun seqname
		  VGet seqname.obj$(0).found, found
		Fend
